steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank fills the slot after "chemical reactions" and before "within molecules." The noun "reactions" needs a modifier that can follow it in post-nominal position, not a finite verb that would make a grammatically broken clause.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'occurred': past-tense finite verb. A finite verb here creates a run-on with no conjunction; cannot modify a noun in post-nominal position. ✗
  • Choice B — 'occurring': present participle. Participial phrases can directly follow and modify a noun ("reactions occurring within molecules"). ✓
  • Choice C — 'occur': base-form finite verb. Same problem as A — no conjunction ties it to the main clause. ✗
  • Choice D — 'will occur': future-tense finite verb. Same structural problem; finite verbs cannot serve as post-nominal modifiers. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
